t: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle have (that is NOT a square)?
e: A non-square rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry: one horizontal and one vertical through the center. Its diagonals are NOT lines of symmetry.

t: Two triangles are put together to make a rectangle. Each triangle has 3 sides. How many sides does the rectangle have?
e: Even though the triangles had 6 sides total, the joined sides are hidden inside. The rectangle has only 4 sides.

t: Which has MORE lines of symmetry: a square or a non-square rectangle?
e: A square has 4 lines of symmetry (horizontal, vertical, and 2 diagonals). A non-square rectangle has only 2 (horizontal and vertical).

t: You cut a square diagonally. What 2 shapes do you get?
e: Cutting a square along a diagonal creates 2 right triangles. Each triangle has the diagonal as its longest side.

t: Emma says the letter S has a line of symmetry. Is she correct?
o: Yes, it has a vertical line
Yes, it has a horizontal line
No, S has no line of symmetry
Yes, it has a diagonal line
e: The letter S has no line of symmetry. If you fold it any way, the halves do not match. (S does have rotational symmetry, but not a line of symmetry.)

t: You arrange 2 small squares into an L-shape. How many sides does the L-shape have?
e: An L-shape made from 2 squares has 6 sides. The shared edge becomes interior, creating extra outside corners.

t: How many lines of symmetry does an equilateral triangle have?
e: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry -- one from each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

t: If you put 2 identical right triangles together along their longest sides, what shape can you make?
e: Two identical right triangles placed together along their longest sides (hypotenuse) form a rectangle.

t: A shape has exactly 1 line of symmetry. Which could it be?
e: An isosceles triangle (2 equal sides) has exactly 1 line of symmetry, from the top vertex straight down to the base.

t: You cut a hexagon into 6 equal triangles from the center. How many sides does each triangle have?
e: Each piece is a triangle, so each has 3 sides -- even though they came from a hexagon.

t: Does the letter H have a line of symmetry?
e: The capital letter H has 2 lines of symmetry: one vertical (left matches right) and one horizontal (top matches bottom).

t: What shape do you get if you put 4 identical squares together in a 2-by-2 grid?
e: Four squares in a 2x2 arrangement make one bigger square. Each side is twice as long as one small square's side.

t: Amy folds a rectangle in half along its line of symmetry (top to bottom). The folded shape is a ___.
e: Folding a rectangle in half along its horizontal center gives a smaller rectangle, half the height of the original.

t: Which shape has MORE lines of symmetry: an equilateral triangle or a non-square rectangle?
e: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry. A non-square rectangle has 2. The triangle has more!

t: You have a big triangle. You draw a line from one side to another, parallel to the base. How many shapes do you now have?
e: Drawing a line parallel to the base cuts the triangle into 2 shapes: a smaller triangle on top and a trapezoid on the bottom.

t: Can you split a rectangle into 2 equal triangles?
o: Yes, by cutting corner to corner diagonally
Only if it is a square
e: Yes! Drawing a diagonal line from one corner to the opposite corner splits any rectangle into 2 equal right triangles.

t: How many lines of symmetry does a regular pentagon (all sides equal) have?
e: A regular pentagon has 5 lines of symmetry -- one from each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

t: Which of these cannot be made by combining 2 identical squares?
o: A bigger shape with 4 or more sides
e: Two squares cannot make a triangle. Squares have right angles and straight sides that combine into rectangles or L-shapes, not triangles.

t: A shape and its mirror image look exactly the same. What must the shape have?
o: At least one line of symmetry
e: If a shape looks the same as its mirror image, it has at least one line of symmetry. The mirror line is the line of symmetry.

t: You arrange 3 equilateral triangles in a row, sharing sides. How many sides does the new shape have?
e: Three triangles in a row (forming a trapezoid-like strip) have 5 outer sides. The shared edges are interior and do not count.
